rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 19: "The CLI auto-loads standalone skills under..." — this is explanation, not action lead-in. Should name what the prompt does in one sentence.
rules_evaluated.fix: Before the **Prompt** label on line 19, add: "Install the authored skill as a standalone CLI tool by copying it to `~/.claude/skills/`."; before line 56, lead-in is already present and correct ("Ask Claude to apply the packaged policy lens..."); before line 83, add: "Ask Claude to apply the agent-security lens to the same system, identify all reachable external systems, and name the top risks for each named attack class."; before line 107, replace "Look at the two reports. Pick one risk to mitigate." with "Tell Claude the specific risk you picked, then ask it to apply a mitigation shape and walk you through what changed."

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa).
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 50: "Do not run a toy verification on one file. The first loaded use is the real audit." — Sutherland's counterintuitive frame. line 99: "The uncomfortable feeling is the evidence." — Rory reframe. line 103: "Not the easiest, not the scariest. The one that bugs you on the second skim. Your gut is data here." — Sutherland behavioral-econ voice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 54: packaged lens earned with back-reference ("comes through the reusable lens you shaped"). line 61–62: verdicts named in context. line 92, 110: mitigation shapes named as (scope, split, filter, gate, review). line 114: residual decision operationalized.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Always 'you', never 'the student'; teacher framing stays out of body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 75: "While the report runs, stay with it." — second-person. line 103: "The one that bugs you..." — student experience naming, not narration of past behavior.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
rules_evaluated.evidence: no maintainer-only vocabulary detected in body prose. 'assessment half of the loop' is operationalized through the exercise flow (line 5 frames the loop).

line 99: "The uncomfortable feeling is the evidence." line 121: "The unease that remains is what the loop is supposed to produce, not what it failed to remove." Both name Module 4's deepened-unease mood without flattening it.

lines 21–28, 56–73, 83–95, 107–117: all contain direct path references (module-4/skills/security-audit/, outputs/policy-report.md) or context references ('the reusable check I authored'). No [BRACKETS] placeholders.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 19–28, 56–73, 83–95, 107–117: all use **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* chip; all have blank lines and proper fence formatting.
line 7 uses semicolon (correct). no em-dashes detected in body prose.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 135: "Absolute certainty is not on offer. The discipline is." is followed immediately by concrete boundary (raw policy first, packaged lenses second, one mitigation, one residual). Carve-out is concrete and directional.
